phenomenon: Dune ecosystem: pioneer plants stabilizing sand
key_measurement: Ammophila arenaria (marram grass) traps vertical sediment accretion at 0.3–1.0 m/year; foredune ridges stabilized by pioneer vegetation can accrete to 3–5 m above high-tide mark within decades (Doing, 1985)

sources.citation: Doing, H. (1985) 'Coastal fore-dune zonation and succession in various parts of the world', Vegetatio 61(1–3):65–75
van der Putten, W.H., Van Dijk, C., Peters, B.A.M. (1993) 'Plant-specific soil-borne diseases contribute to succession in foredune vegetation', Nature 362:53–56
USDA NRCS Plant Guide — American Beachgrass (Ammophila breviligulata Fern.), USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service

visualization_compliance: Dune zonation sequence must be accurate: embryo dune → foredune → yellow dune → grey dune → dune heath/slack — label each zone
Plant species shown must match their documented geographic range (Ammophila arenaria in Europe; A. breviligulata in North America — do not conflate)
Root system depth for Ammophila must reflect published data (~1–2 m vertical rhizome extent)
Do not show climax woodland on active foredunes — succession timescale is decades to centuries
Soil horizon development (incipient humification in grey dune) must be directionally correct
Label ILLUSTRATIVE on cross-section diagrams; do not depict species outside documented habitat tolerance
